title: Xanthan White Indian Rice Biryani
description: A rich, springy, cozy Indian (Rajasthani) dish featuring white vinegar, slow-cooked to perfection with a raw-friendly twist, served in the form of a biryani.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of ghee in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the onion, ginger, and garlic and saut7 them until the onion is translucent.
Add the basmati rice and stir to coat the rice with the ghee and mix with the onion mixture.
Add 4 cups of water and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 15 minutes or until the rice is cooked.
While the rice is cooking, heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add the xanthan gum and stir to combine.
Add 2 tablespoons of white vinegar and stir to combine.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
To assemble the biryani, place a layer of cooked rice in the bottom of a serving dish.
Sprinkle a layer of the xanthan gum mixture on top of the rice.
Repeat the layers of rice and xanthan gum mixture 2-3 times, ending with a layer of rice on top.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and raw cashews, if desired.
